title: Simplify the release tarball packaging now that the UI is embedded
action: In the release workflow's Package step, remove the copy of the static assets directory into the packaged bundle — the binary embeds the UI at compile time (embed-pkg-2), so the tarball no longer needs a separate static assets directory. Keep everything else in the bundle (binary, install.sh, config.example.json, README.md, packaging/, docs) since install.sh still reads packaging/herdr-gateway.service and config.example.json from those files when run from an extracted tarball or a checkout.

truths: The packaged tarball/zip no longer contains a static assets directory

artifacts.substantive: Package step no longer copies the static assets directory

prohibitions: install.sh, config.example.json, packaging/, README.md, docs/*.md stay in the bundle

trace.outcome: Removed static asset copy from release package step; UI is now embedded at compile-time
